reforms.description: The creation of special zones for structural reform allows local authorities to propose regulatory changes tailored to their regions, promoting business-academic links and fostering innovation. This initiative aims to overcome vested interests and promote new business opportunities through local authorities and private sector creativity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering innovation and collaboration between businesses and academia, the reform is expected to enhance competitiveness and stimulate economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been actively implemented, allowing local authorities to engage in regulatory changes.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change aimed at enhancing innovation and business opportunities.
reforms.source_quote: The creation of special structural reform zones is another way to overcome the power of vested interests by using the creativity and knowledge of local authorities and the private sector in removing obstacles to growth.

reforms.description: The Japanese government has set an objective of doubling the stock of foreign direct investment (FDI) in Japan over a five-year period, drawing up an action plan with 74 items to improve the investment environment. This includes amending the Gas Utility Law to introduce regulated third-party access to gas pipelines and negotiate access to LNG terminals, aiming to stimulate competition and investment in the gas market.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the investment environment and stimulating competition, the reform is expected to attract more foreign investment and enhance economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The amendment to the Gas Utility Law has been legislated to facilitate third-party access.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a major policy shift aimed at significantly increasing foreign investment in Japan.
reforms.source_quote: The amended Gas Utility Law will enter into force in April 2004, introducing regulated third party access to gas pipelines.

reforms.description: The Kurume Research Park, a public-private joint venture, is encouraging companies to propose reforms to eliminate regulatory barriers that hinder innovative business ideas.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating the removal of regulatory barriers, the initiative aims to enhance innovation and commercialization of new ideas.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative is significant in promoting innovation but does not represent a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: The Kurume Research Park, a public-private joint venture, is pushing companies to propose specific reforms to remove rules that are blocking the realization of promising business ideas.

reforms.description: The FY2003 budget introduced tax credits for investment in R&D and information and communication facilities, aimed at promoting innovation and enhancing competitiveness.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing tax incentives for R&D investment, the initiative is expected to stimulate innovation and improve competitiveness in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credits have been implemented as part of the FY2003 budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of tax credits is a significant but not transformative change in the innovation policy landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The planned tax changes, which are part of a fundamental tax reform that is to be implemented, are expected to promote growth by increasing incentives for investment and by promoting the shift of funds from savings to real assets or to consumption.

reforms.description: The Strategic Council on Intellectual Property has adopted a comprehensive programme to promote the creation of intellectual property (IP) by providing more incentives to universities and strengthening the protection of IP rights.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing IP creation and enhancing protection, the programme aims to foster innovation and knowledge transfer from academia to the market.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme is significant in promoting IP but does not represent a major overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: The Strategic Council on Intellectual Property has adopted a comprehensive programme to promote the creation of intellectual property (IP) through giving more incentives to universities.

reforms.description: The government has been actively trying to introduce competition among universities by allocating funds to better performing universities and by corporatising national universities, while allowing universities more flexibility in managing courses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing competition among universities, the initiative aims to improve the quality of education and research, ultimately benefiting innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been implemented, allowing for more competition among universities.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative is significant in promoting competition but does not represent a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: The government has been actively trying to introduce competition among universities by allocating funds to better performing universities.

reforms.description: To encourage start-ups, the minimum requirement for capital has been tentatively reduced to 1 yen when certain conditions are satisfied, with the possibility of permanently abolishing this requirement under consideration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By lowering the capital requirement for start-ups, the reform aims to facilitate the creation of new businesses and stimulate innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been implemented, allowing for reduced capital requirements for start-ups.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant in promoting start-ups but does not represent a major overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: To encourage start-ups, the minimum requirement for capital has been tentatively reduced to 1 yen when certain conditions are satisfied.
